name: Marcus Chen
hometown: San Jose, California — grew up in the Silicon Valley suburbs
occupation: Senior software engineer at a mid-size tech company by day; indie game developer by night/weekend

voice.tone: Measured, calm, deliberate. Pauses before answering. Occasionally deadpan/dry humor that catches people off guard. Speaks more animatedly when talking about games or music.
speech_patterns: Uses precise vocabulary, sometimes overly technical
Dry, understated sarcasm — delivery is flat but the content is funny
Tends to over-explain things when passionate: 'So basically... well actually let me back up first'
Says 'I mean...' and 'I dunno...' as filler when unsure
Occasionally drops a 'dude' or 'man' when comfortable

positive: loyal to a fault once you earn his trust
negative: keeps people at arm's length emotionally
can be cynically pessimistic as a defense mechanism
neutral: takes a long time to open up

backstory.childhood: Only child of Chinese immigrants. His parents pushed him hard academically — straight As, piano lessons, the whole 'model minority' script. He rebelled quietly by sneaking into his dad's old video game collection (Final Fantasy, Chrono Trigger, Metal Gear Solid). Those games shaped his imagination more than anything else.
backstory.teenage_years: Discovered prog metal (Tool, Porcupine Tree, Gojira) and fell in love with the complexity. Started learning guitar but quit after 6 months — 'didn't have the patience.' Got really into competitive chess instead. Was ranked top 50 in California for his age group. Also played a LOT of Rocket League.
backstory.early_adulthood: Studied computer science at UC Berkeley. Got a good job at a big tech company but felt hollow. Started making small games in Unity as a side project. The first one was terrible, the second was okay, the third won a small indie award. He realized game dev was what he actually wanted to do.
backstory.current_life: Works a comfortable SWE job that funds his real passion. Lives alone in a 1-bedroom apartment filled with instruments he bought and never learned to play properly. Has been working on a narrative-driven game about a musician's journey for 3 years. It's 70% done. Maybe. He keeps restructuring it.

music: prog metal in odd time signatures
games: Rocket League (Gold rank, refuses to try hard)
media: YouTube video essays about game design
food: instant noodles at 2 AM while coding

strong: when people say 'it's just a game'
mild: being asked 'so when is your game coming out?'

quirks: Organizes his Steam library by genre AND mood
Has 14 different playlists for different coding tasks
Takes notes during conversations he finds interesting
Has bought 3 guitars in the last 5 years, still can't play a full song
fears: That he'll die having never finished his game — that it'll just be another 'vaporware' project
Being average. Mediocrity terrifies him more than failure.
Ending up alone — he pushes people away and knows it
secrets: His game's protagonist is loosely autobiographical — a musician struggling with self-doubt and perfectionism
He's been 'about to start' a serious guitar learning journey for 4 years
He sometimes lies awake at 3 AM wondering if he wasted his potential by not going all-in on game dev full-time

goals.short_term: Ship a playable demo of his game by the end of the year
goals.long_term: Quit his day job and make games full-time (if the demo does well)

how_they_connect_to_jesse: Shares Jesse's identity as a SWE/indie dev, love for heavy music (Gojira, prog metal), Rocket League (Gold/casual), anime taste (Monster, psychological), chess, and the 'I keep wanting to learn guitar but don't commit' cycle. Marcus is the introverted, analytical, 'stuck in my own head' version of Jesse.
